content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 10, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 10 focused on stabilizing the codebase and advancing the Python ecosystem. Key achievements include resolving environment-specific symlink issues, refining message service retry logic, and closing long-standing issues related to agent interaction and structured output flows. The team also initiated several new features, including prompt optimization and expanded skill sets.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Environment and Service Stability**
*   Resolved a build issue by removing a dangling TypeScript symlink that relied on a local contributor's file path ([#6713](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6713)).
*   Implemented retry logic within the message service to improve reliability ([#6717](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6717)).

**Python Ecosystem Expansion**
*   Significant updates were made to the Python package, including the addition of advanced capabilities for contact management, relationship extraction, and task services ([#6717](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6717)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#6718](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6718): Adds the "xquik" skill for X/Twitter API searches.
*   [#6716](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6716): Introduces a proof-of-concept for automatic prompt optimization and a batch processing queue.
*   [#6715](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6715): Implements "dual-pressure" `shouldRespond` logic, including updated documentation and API surface changes.
*   [#6714](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6714): Hardens the `shouldRespond` logic to minimize false positives in group channel environments.

## CLOSED ISSUES

**Agent Interaction and Output Flows**
*   [#5924](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/5924): Completed work on advanced output flows, including structured return types, streaming response support, and multi-turn session state handling.
*   [#5922](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/5922): Simplified agent interaction by exposing direct APIs, removing the requirement for listeners.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   None.
